item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely beautiful and heartbreaking. Upvoted.

- a paleontology lover

item.title: Happy Mother's Day
item.content:encoded:
And now (depending on your country, most of) you realize the significance of the date in the addendum.

<3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, sweet, and touching. Thanks for the tears.
part of this sadness may be due to Land Before Time feels idk

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There now exists a tale for this SCP, by yours truly: Ramah

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Daww

Happy ending(?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem! I love this SCP and I think you deserve 100 arts, and I will make sure that you get to see all of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, knowing that there's such thing as #dinoween has convinced me it's worth it to dust off my tumblr again. Thanks for making me aware of this :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have more art! It was for the dinoween ghost theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this is a amazing article. It was short, sweet, and the final addendum packed a huge punch.

Well deserved +1 for a well baked skip. (i regret that analogy)

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Woohoo! Thanks again, everyone who read and voted on this one!
It's about time, congrats on making it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaaaaw! Every SCP that gives me feels deserves my +1

item.title: Re: A few things
item.content:encoded:
"It" in that first sentence refers to the nest, not the eggs. Not sure how I can make that any clearer.

As for the vicinity thing, I suppose I could add the approximate radius where the wind is happening, but I didn't want to overload on details that specific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only anomalous thing is that I never read this thing before now. +1 with HIGH ENERGY!!!

item.title: A few things
item.content:encoded: Just a little feedback after reading the article (loved it btw), I noticed that you tended to use the word It a lot, which tends to take a bit away from the experience. For example, instead of saying "SCP-3637-1 lay down next to it and nuzzled the fossilized eggs, before collapsing into salt crystals.", You can say "SCP-3637-1 lay down next to and nuzzled the fossilized eggs, before collapsing." Not to mention, it would be a them, not an it, as there are multiple eggs. Also "During a 3637-Ramah event, wind speeds in the vicinity of SCP-3637 will increase to around 140 kph, a loud roar will be heard," What is the vicinity, first of all, and I would recommend saying some variation of "produced a loud roar", instead of a loud roar will be heard. Other than my Nitpicking, the article is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… Why is a dinosaur fossil SCP making me cry?

WHY?!

+1, man. You seriously deserve it. It's hard to make me cry, so you did a good job of making this fossilized herbivorous dinosaur cause me to weep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A beautiful story. I translated it into Chinese. Here.

（and +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(I'm secretly harvesting tears of readers and evaporating them to stockpile salt for another dino)

I love cool stuff but can't usually write strong emotion well, so no way I was gonna pass up this chance to combine them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sad dinosaur is sad. Damn you Weryllium for making something so cool (dinosaurs) into something so emotionally devastating (dinosaurs and their children).

+1 for unrestrained DinoFeels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit late, but noting that this page has been translated for the Japanese branch by C-Dives and can be read here. Thank you, translators, it's really appreciated! You guys are awesome!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really glad you liked it!

Deep down I've always wanted to write an SCP inspired by the Song of Solomon passage, but could never find the right concept. I made the connection to the Jeremiah verse quite a bit later on, when I wanted a thematic subdesignation for the event.

Maiasaura fossils are often found in herds and communal nests; my personal canon is that the mother was caught in a massive flood while out searching for plants to cover the nest in, and this flood was big enough to wipe out the colony as well. I admit to a tiny bit of guesswork as to the distance between her body and the nest, but if anything, the plot convenience is that only her nest was recovered, which is easily fixed if you imagine that the rest of the nests are also close by, dug up but not included in this file because they're not important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the scripture use; that's a particularly evocative passage that is used a lot in music and literature.

Entry was short, (super) sweet, and to the point; I'm not even mad that is exceedingly unlikely this creature and her nest would both be fossilized, separately (I think; I'm not paleontological expert).

Great work, +1, wish I could upvote it more.

item.title: *squee*
item.content:encoded: A dinosaur-sized thank you to SunnyClockwork for making this amazing art! It's awesome and you're awesome, Sunny! And you're awesome too, person reading this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mission accomplished! Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda figured that was what it was doing, the addendum confirmed it, and ooh, ouch, you got me.

item.title: And the award for Mom of the Epoch goes to...
item.content:encoded:
“A voice was heard in Ramah,
Lamentation and bitter weeping,
Rachel weeping for her children,
Refusing to be comforted for her children,
Because they are no more.”

~ Jeremiah 31:15

Many waters cannot quench love,
Nor can the floods drown it.

~ Song of Solomon 8:7

And so today we honor the noble Maiasaura, the Good Mother Lizard.

Thanks to FortuneFavorsBold for all their help during the concept phase of this, as well as to A Random Day, MrWrong, Mortos, DrBleep, and The Great Hippo for reading the draft.

Image is CC-BY-SA 3.0, sourced from here, edited by me. I cropped it to remove the kids in the foreground.

Kudos to whoever finds the hidden easter dino egg.
